aboutGame.title: What is Conquest of Azeroth?
paragraphs: Conquest of Azeroth is a free-to-play custom Classic+ MMORPG realm within Project Ascension. It reimagines Vanilla Azeroth with 21 original lore-driven classes, custom resources, new spell visuals, unique mechanics, and deeply customizable talent trees.

Players select a specialization at level 10, develop their character through separate class and specialization talent trees, and progress toward level 60. Endgame content includes expanded dungeons, Mythic+, multi-difficulty raids, arenas, world bosses, custom zones, and optional high-risk PvP.

explore.title: Explore Conquest of Azeroth Guides
explore.description: Jump into Conquest of Azeroth guides, release notes, access help, classes, builds, rankings, addons, and community resources.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Access
modules.description: Start with account setup, launcher installation, realm selection, PTR download notes, and free-to-play access basics.

highlights.detail: Create or confirm your Ascension account before installing the launcher.
Download the PC launcher and select the Conquest of Azeroth realm.
Review PTR and free-to-play notes before choosing your first character.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Beginner Guide
modules.description: Start Conquest of Azeroth with account setup, launcher installation, realm selection, class choice, first quests, interface setup, and early talent decisions.

highlights.detail: Create an Ascension account, install the launcher, and choose the Conquest of Azeroth realm from the client.
Pick a class after reviewing its combat role, resource flow, difficulty, and solo-friendly tools.
Reach level 10, select a specialization, and begin spending points across class and specialization talent trees.
Follow level-appropriate quests and dungeons while replacing weak gear with useful custom equipment.
Prepare for level 60 by saving build notes, learning dungeon mechanics, and choosing a PvE or PvP focus.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Class Tier List
modules.description: Compare Conquest of Azeroth classes by beginner comfort, group demand, PvE damage or healing value, tank utility, PvP pressure, and build complexity.

highlights.label: S
highlights.detail: Low-friction leveling, strong dungeon utility, and clear talent progression for a first character.

highlights.label: A
highlights.detail: Strong group value with a few build decisions to learn before raids, Mythic+, or arenas.

highlights.label: B
highlights.detail: Rewarding picks that become smoother with gear, dungeon knowledge, or tighter rotation planning.

highlights.label: C
highlights.detail: Niche PvP or encounter-specific builds that need careful preparation before they feel consistent.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Classes and Specializations
modules.description: Explore the 21 original classes and 69 specializations that define Conquest of Azeroth, including custom resources, spell visuals, level 10 specialization choices, and role-defining mechanics.

highlights.label: 21 Classes
highlights.detail: Choose from 21 original lore-driven classes built for Classic Azeroth roles, resources, and combat styles.

highlights.label: 69 Specs
highlights.detail: At level 10, each character chooses a specialization that reshapes talents, role options, and combat identity.

highlights.label: Resources
highlights.detail: Custom class resources and spell visuals make each class feel distinct from standard Classic roles.

highlights.label: Dual Trees
highlights.detail: Class and specialization talent trees work together, giving each build a clear leveling and endgame path.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Leveling and Gear
modules.description: Plan Conquest of Azeroth progression from level 1 to 60 with quest routing, dungeon readiness, talent pacing, gear upgrades, and endgame preparation.

highlights.detail: Start with a class whose resource system and role match your preferred solo or group pace.
Quest through familiar Classic Azeroth zones while using custom class tools to smooth weak spots.
Spend talent points regularly because early passives shape damage, healing, mitigation, and resource flow.
Replace outdated weapons, armor, and trinkets before queueing into harder dungeon content.
At level 60, prepare separate PvE and PvP gear sets for dungeons, raids, arenas, or Hybrid Risk.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Talents and Builds
modules.description: Plan Conquest of Azeroth builds around class talents, specialization trees, custom resources, rotations, dungeon roles, raid utility, and PvP pressure.

highlights.label: Class Tree
highlights.detail: Class talents define core combat tools, resource flow, utility buttons, and early leveling comfort.

highlights.label: Spec Tree
highlights.detail: Specialization talents sharpen your chosen role and turn level 10 choices into long-term build identity.

highlights.label: Rotations
highlights.detail: Build planning should connect talents, cooldown timing, resource spenders, and encounter movement.

highlights.label: Loadouts
highlights.detail: Keep separate setups for leveling, dungeons, raids, arenas, and high-risk world content.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth Dungeons and Raids
modules.description: Prepare for Conquest of Azeroth dungeon routing, Mythic+ progression, multi-difficulty raids, world bosses, and group roles after level 60.

highlights.label: Mythic+
highlights.detail: Mythic+ dungeons reward route knowledge, interrupt discipline, defensive timing, and clean role coordination.

highlights.label: Raids
highlights.detail: Multi-difficulty raids ask players to refine builds, gear, consumables, and encounter assignments.

highlights.label: World Bosses
highlights.detail: World boss preparation centers on survivability, group utility, positioning, and quick build adjustments.

highlights.label: Manastorm
highlights.detail: The Manastorm gives endgame players another target for build testing, group planning, and rewards.

modules.name: Conquest of Azeroth PvP and Endgame Systems
modules.description: Track Conquest of Azeroth arena preparation, Hybrid Risk decisions, custom zones, world objectives, and build swaps for late-game play.

highlights.label: Arena
highlights.detail: Arena builds need burst windows, crowd control plans, defensive cooldowns, and matchup-aware talent choices.

highlights.label: Hybrid Risk
highlights.detail: Hybrid Risk activities reward players who can balance stronger rewards against open-world danger.

highlights.label: Custom Zones
highlights.detail: Custom zones expand the endgame loop with new objectives, group opportunities, and build checks.

highlights.label: Build Swaps
highlights.detail: Late-game players benefit from swapping talents, gear, and utility tools between PvE, PvP, and world content.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.description: Quick answers to the most common questions about Conquest of Azeroth.

items.question: What is Conquest of Azeroth?
items.answer: Conquest of Azeroth is a custom Classic+ MMORPG realm within Project Ascension with 21 original classes, 69 specializations, custom talents, and expanded endgame systems.

items.question: Is Conquest of Azeroth official World of Warcraft?
items.answer: No. Conquest of Azeroth belongs to the Project Ascension ecosystem, and this wiki is an independent fan-made guide hub rather than an official Blizzard release.

items.question: How should beginners start Conquest of Azeroth?
items.answer: Beginners should create an Ascension account, install the launcher, choose the Conquest of Azeroth realm, pick a class, and follow a level 1-60 route while learning talents and gear basics.

items.question: Which Conquest of Azeroth class should beginners pick?
items.answer: Beginners in Conquest of Azeroth should start with a class that has a clear role, forgiving resource flow, useful solo tools, and a specialization that matches their preferred dungeon, raid, or PvP path.

items.question: How many classes and specializations are in Conquest of Azeroth?
items.answer: Conquest of Azeroth features 21 original classes and 69 specializations, with more than 1000 spells and talents across the custom class system.

items.question: What level cap does Conquest of Azeroth use?
items.answer: Conquest of Azeroth uses a level 60 cap and builds its progression around class talents, specialization talents, dungeons, raids, arenas, world bosses, and optional high-risk PvP.

items.question: What endgame content does Conquest of Azeroth include?
items.answer: Conquest of Azeroth endgame content includes Mythic+ dungeons, multi-difficulty raids, arenas, world bosses, custom zones, the Manastorm, and Hybrid Risk activities.

items.question: Where can players find access and setup help?
items.answer: Use the access section for launcher setup, free-to-play notes, PTR download guidance, and how-to-play details for Conquest of Azeroth.

items.question: Where can players follow Conquest of Azeroth updates?
items.answer: Players can follow Conquest of Azeroth updates through the Project Ascension website, official Discord, YouTube channel, X account, Reddit community, server status page, and Ascension Database.

finalCta.title: Ready to Conquer Azeroth?
finalCta.description: From choosing your first class and planning a level 1 route to mastering level 60 builds, Mythic+ dungeons, raids, arenas, and world bosses, this community wiki helps you prepare for every stage of the journey.

privacyPolicy.title: Privacy Policy
privacyPolicy.description: Privacy Policy for Conquest of Azeroth Wiki - how we handle visitor data and privacy on this fan-made guide hub.
content: This fan wiki provides informational game guides for Conquest of Azeroth. We do not request account credentials, launcher passwords, Discord credentials, or private payment information.
Basic analytics, advertising, and hosting providers may process standard technical information such as device type, browser, approximate region, and visited pages.
External links may lead to Project Ascension, Discord, YouTube, Reddit, X, or other Conquest of Azeroth community destinations. Those services are governed by their own privacy policies.

about.title: About
about.description: About Conquest of Azeroth Wiki - an independent fan-built guide hub covering classes, builds, leveling, talents, gear, dungeons, raids, PvP, and endgame systems.
content: Conquest of Azeroth Wiki is an independent fan-built guide hub covering classes, builds, leveling, talents, gear, dungeons, raids, PvP, and endgame systems.

The site links players to official Project Ascension destinations including the website, Discord, YouTube, Reddit, X, and the Ascension Database while keeping fan guide content separate from official channels.

termsOfService.title: Terms of Service
termsOfService.description: Terms of Service for Conquest of Azeroth Wiki - usage terms for this independent fan-made guide hub.
content: This site is an independent fan-made guide hub. Content is provided for informational and entertainment purposes only.
Game systems, classes, builds, talents, dungeons, raids, PvP, and update details are covered as informational guide topics for Conquest of Azeroth players.
By using this site, you agree not to misuse it, attempt unauthorized access, or present this fan wiki as an official Project Ascension, Blizzard Entertainment, or World of Warcraft property.

copyright.title: Copyright
copyright.description: Copyright information for Conquest of Azeroth Wiki - ownership and takedown details.
content: Conquest of Azeroth, Project Ascension, Warcraft, World of Warcraft, Blizzard Entertainment branding, logos, game media, and related materials belong to their respective owners.
This site is a non-official fan wiki implementation for educational, reference, and guide presentation purposes.
If you own rights to content displayed here and have a concern, please contact the site operator for review.
